purpose.summary: The CBPAUP0C program deletes authorization details and summary records from an IMS database. It takes checkpoints during processing and abends if errors occur during deletion or checkpointing.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of a larger authorization processing system, responsible for removing pending authorization requests.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph deletes an authorization detail segment (PAUTDTL1) from the IMS database. It uses the PAUT-PCB-NUM to specify the PCB and the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS as the source of the data to be deleted. A debug display is shown if DEBUG-ON is set. After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful. If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments WS-NO-DTL-DELETED. If the deletion fails (DIBSTAT is not spaces), it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It simply contains an EXIT statement, returning control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph deletes an authorization summary segment (PAUTSUM0) from the IMS database. It uses the PAUT-PCB-NUM to specify the PCB and the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY as the source of the data to be deleted. A debug display is shown if DEBUG-ON is set. After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful. If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments WS-NO-SUMRY-DELETED. If the deletion fails (DIBSTAT is not spaces), it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It simply contains an EXIT statement, returning control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph takes an IMS checkpoint using the WK-CHKPT-ID. After the checkpoint operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the checkpoint was successful. If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments WS-NO-CHKP. If WS-NO-CHKP is greater than or equal to P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ, it resets WS-NO-CHKP to 0 and displays a checkpoint success message including WS-NO-SUMRY-READ and WS-CURR-APP-ID. If the checkpoint fails (DIBSTAT is not spaces), it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT, WS-NO-SUMRY-READ, and WS-CURR-APP-ID and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT paragraph. It simply contains an EXIT statement, returning control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph handles program termination due to an error. It displays a message indicating that CBPAUP0C is abending. It then sets the RETURN-CODE to 16 and executes a GOBACK statement to terminate the program.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It simply contains an EXIT statement, returning control to the calling paragraph.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT NOT = SPACES (Authorization Detail Delete Failed)
DIBSTAT NOT = SPACES (Authorization Summary Delete Failed)

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of WK-CHKPT-ID?
open_questions.context: The code uses WK-CHKPT-ID in the checkpoint call, but its origin and meaning are unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the data definitions to understand its format and intended use.

open_questions.question: What is the structure of PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS and PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY?
open_questions.context: The code deletes segments from these structures, but their fields and copybooks are not defined in the provided snippet.
open_questions.suggestion: Check the program's data division for copybook inclusions that define these structures.

open_questions.context: The program's calling context is unknown.
